codeReview.summary: The worker verified the right suites and the current working tree does contain deterministic test changes for `file-index.test.ts`, but the claimed commit is unrelated and does not include either reviewed test file. Because the success handoff points to commit `17a0c1f`, scrutiny cannot trust that the claimed timeout-baseline stabilization was actually captured in committed history.

issues.description: The feature handoff claims commit `17a0c1f620f7a11df4bcf476fdfdc38264762446` implemented the timeout-baseline stabilization, but `git show`/`git diff --stat` for that commit contain no changes to `src/lib/core/services/file-index.test.ts` or `src/lib/features/editor/services/save-reload-hardening.test.ts`. The reviewed stabilization exists only as working-tree state, so the feature is not trustworthy as a committed milestone artifact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The stability-hardening worker transcript reported success with `returnToOrchestrator: false` and reused the previous assistant commit ID even though the reviewed test edits were not part of that commit and the repository was already dirty. The worker/worker-base procedure appears not to guard against claiming success on uncommitted working-tree-only fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `2026-03-25T10-06-29-154Z__stabilize-document-core-timeout-test-baseline__060f0a82-7f0f-4583-a097-492756f35c96.json` records commitId `17a0c1f...`; `git show --stat 17a0c1f...` only changes assistant files, while `git status --short -- src/lib/core/services/file-index.test.ts src/lib/features/editor/services/save-reload-hardening.test.ts` still shows `M`/`??` for the reviewed test files.

summary: Fail: the timeout-stabilization behavior appears present in the current working tree and was reportedly validated, but the feature’s claimed commit is unrelated to the reviewed files. Because scrutiny needs trustworthy diff/commit evidence for milestone validation, this feature cannot pass until the actual test changes are captured in the recorded feature artifact.
